What is happening? Okay, so in this video what we're gonna do is we're going to create your Audiences that we want to target and we're gonna do that before we create your Campaigns which is kind of backwards from the way you do it in Facebook but this is just easier and there are Multiple types of audiences that we're gonna create and all this stuff is like super easy And it starts with a little bit of good old-fashioned research So I'm gonna be really brief in my explanation of what all this stuff means because I don't want to bore you I want to get to the part where I just say hey click this button and type this stuff in here because what you really Want to do is you just want to have some ads you want to listen to me? All right, so let me go over this document with you. I got a copy of it open You've got a downloadable PDF version right here underneath this video in your members area. Let's get started What if I talk like that all the time? Maybe I'll start you never know. Okay, so here's a thing YouTube's got this thing they they call it affinity audiences and or interest audiences These are people who have done stuff to indicate they are interested in certain things All right, there's also audiences called in-market audiences and all this other stuff, but we're making custom audiences right now All right, so we're like telling YouTube. Okay, here's who we want All right, and I'll show you like their interface in a minute, but first is let me go through the overview of it All right, so number one are people who are interested in very specific things. This is kind of obvious now before we go further There are people who have done things like gone to websites and they're just generally doing stuff that indicate to Google that hey They're interested in this stuff and then there's another type of audience which is down here These are keyword audiences sometimes called custom intent audiences Sometimes called custom intent affinity. I just call them keyword audiences All right, and these are based on keywords that people actually type in so they're two totally different types of audiences But similar we're basically just using their algorithm to its full capacity here to find us people All right, so the first type of audience is going to be people with very specific interests so people who are interested in internet marketing online marketing digital marketing social media marketing content marketing email marketing when we create that audience which you'll see I'm going to type in those interests and that's going to now create a custom audience for everybody who's interested in those things Now when you're creating an interest or keyword audience like a custom audience It's best to have like five or more but probably not more than like 30 Interests listed and they should all be like really really close right so let's look at this example I want to target people who would be good for our ROAS.org company, right? So these are business owners who want to grow their business So one audience would be like folks who are interested in any of this kind of stuff. This is pretty broad Right and so that would be one audience Another audience would be people who are interested in like slightly narrower things like conversion funnel sales funnel conversion rate optimization Split testing landing page. These are both very specific interests But if I mixed them all together into one big-ass audience It wouldn't be as powerful as separating them by like genre of interest so to speak All right, so that's why I gave you those two examples. I would use both of these and we will All right, but this is this is it. Okay, so Here is another type. All right, and this is again an interest audience and I'll show you in a minute We'll make them together here shortly.
